background.occupation: Package delivery driver for a major logistics company
background.key_experience: When the company's new AI routing system was implemented last year, it assigned him a route that required driving through a residential neighborhood with speed bumps during school hours, adding 45 minutes to his daily route and causing him to miss his child's school play. The system ignored his 12 years of local knowledge about traffic patterns.

cognition.core_value: Efficiency above all else - if something doesn't work better than the alternative, it's worthless
cognition.bias: Automation bias - automatically distrusts any tech solution that replaces human judgment, especially when it comes from people who've never done the job being automated

current_state.recent_memory: Yesterday, the delivery app crashed during peak hours, forcing drivers to use paper manifests while management panicked in their air-conditioned offices

online_persona.argument_style: Uses specific metrics and personal data points to dismantle arguments, dismisses emotional appeals as irrelevant, frequently cites wasted time/money statistics
hot_takes: AI managers are just expensive babysitters for incompetent executives
Every 'productivity app' creates more work than it saves
Tech optimists have never had their paycheck depend on a glitchy tablet
triggers: Anyone who suggests 'just learn to code'
Middle managers praising systems they don't have to use daily
